pi_items.verified_value: Three PNG screenshot files at /home/ga/algorithm_levelN.png for N=1,2,3
pi_items.source: task.json; verified through GCompris file structure and task script setup_task.sh which references these exact paths

pi_items.verified_value: 50000 bytes (50KB) is reasonable for GCompris screenshots. Reference: GCompris official screenshot (algorithm.png, 400x260) is 13.5KB; full-size screenshots at 800x520 or larger would be 50-500KB depending on resolution and compression.
pi_items.source: Analysis of GCompris screenshot file sizes from https://www.gcompris.net/screenshots_qt/small/algorithm.png and calculation of expected sizes for interactive task screenshots

pi_items.verified_value: The 'algorithm' activity exists in GCompris and is officially described as 'Logical associations' - a pattern completion activity where users complete fruit sequences by identifying and applying learned patterns
pi_items.source: GitHub KDE/gcompris repository (/src/activities/algorithm) and official GCompris website (https://www.gcompris.net/screenshots-en.html)

pi_items.metadata_value: GCompris is referenced in the task description
pi_items.verified_value: GCompris is a legitimate, real, open-source educational software suite containing 190+ activities for children aged 2-10. Official website: https://www.gcompris.net. GitHub repository: https://github.com/KDE/gcompris. Installable via Ubuntu/Debian packages (gcompris-qt).
pi_items.source: Official GCompris website, GNU Project, Wikipedia, GitHub KDE repository, and package repositories

privileged_info_summary: GCompris is a real, open-source educational software with 190+ activities. The 'algorithm' activity exists and is a pattern completion task where users identify and complete fruit sequences. The task expects three PNG screenshots from GCompris with minimum file size of 50KB each, which is reasonable for interactive screenshots. Data is not synthetic—it is generated through real interaction with GCompris during task execution.
